title: Enable port bindings
body:
Set up nbetworking ports for the following use cases:

* bind the same port between host and container
* bind a specific host port to a different container port
* bind a random host port to a specific container port

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body: :boom: Invalid `.papr.yml`: failed to parse 1st testsuite: repo 1 missing key 'name'.
:boom: Invalid `.papr.yml`: failed to parse 1st testsuite: repo 1 missing key 'name'.
:boom: Invalid `.papr.yml`: failed to parse 1st testsuite: repo 1 missing key 'name'.

I am concerned that you have written a hole bunch of code that is not necessary.

Doesn't

		l, err := net.Listen("tcp", ":0") 

Give you a random port that is open?
@rhatdan I will double check that usage.  The port given must be in a certain range as described by */proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_local_port_range*.  If that is the case, then your suggestion is ideal.
this PR is on hold until containernetworking-cni 0.6 is in stable fedora.  that should have happened last week but until it is, we are stuck.
@rhatdan the suggestion about net.listen was perfect. i have reworked the code as such.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 4d70a69) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
